title: Uzbekistan & Turkmenistan Tour
icon_label: Tashkent, Samarkand, Bukhara, Khiva, Darvaza, Ashgabat, Ancient Merv

labels.label: This Uzbekistan and Turkmenistan tour connects two of Central Asia’s most powerful Silk Road stories. The route begins with Uzbekistan’s historic cities — Tashkent, Samarkand, Bukhara and Khiva — then continues into Turkmenistan for Darvaza Gas Crater, Ashgabat and Ancient Merv.

It is ideal for travelers who want a strong cultural journey with a desert extension. Uzbekistan brings architecture, bazaars and old cities, while Turkmenistan adds remote landscapes, archaeological depth and one of the region’s most unusual travel experiences.

details.label: Local English-speaking guides according to the itinerary
Accommodation in selected hotels and desert camp/yurt-style stay where applicable
Airport and border transfers according to the final route
Entrance tickets for listed sightseeing places
Visa, border and travel planning guidance

images.alt: Silk Road cities and desert routes

details.title: Arrival in Tashkent, Uzbekistan
details.content: Arrive in Tashkent and meet your local guide. Begin with a gentle introduction to Uzbekistan’s capital, including modern avenues, old city areas, local markets and cultural landmarks.

details.title: Tashkent – Samarkand
details.content: Travel to Samarkand, one of the great cities of the Silk Road. Visit Registan Square, major mausoleums, mosques and historic monuments that show the city’s importance in Central Asian history.

details.title: Samarkand Sightseeing
details.content: Continue exploring Samarkand with visits to additional landmarks, local bazaars and craft areas. This day allows a deeper understanding of the city’s architecture, culture and Silk Road heritage.

details.title: Samarkand – Bukhara
details.content: Travel to Bukhara, a city known for its old town atmosphere, madrasahs, caravanserais, mosques and traditional craft culture. Enjoy an evening walk through the historic center.

details.title: Bukhara Sightseeing
details.content: Explore Bukhara’s main highlights, including historic squares, religious monuments, trading domes and old streets. The city offers one of the most atmospheric Silk Road experiences in Uzbekistan.

details.title: Bukhara – Khiva
details.content: Travel across desert landscapes toward Khiva. Arrive at the preserved walled city, where old palaces, minarets and narrow lanes create an open-air museum feeling.

details.title: Khiva Sightseeing
details.content: Explore Itchan Kala, Khiva’s historic inner city. Visit palaces, madrasahs, minarets and viewpoints while learning about the city’s desert setting and role in regional trade routes.

details.title: Khiva – Turkmenistan Border – Darvaza
details.content: Cross into Turkmenistan and continue toward the Karakum Desert. Travel to Darvaza Gas Crater and experience the famous burning crater after sunset.

details.title: Darvaza – Ashgabat
details.content: After breakfast in the desert, return to Ashgabat. Begin exploring Turkmenistan’s capital, known for marble architecture, monuments, wide avenues and a distinctive modern identity.

details.title: Ashgabat City Tour
details.content: Visit Ashgabat’s main highlights, including key monuments, museums, local markets and architectural landmarks. This day gives context to Turkmenistan’s culture, history and modern image.

details.title: Ashgabat – Ancient Merv – Mary
details.content: Travel to the historic region of Mary and visit Ancient Merv, one of the great cities of the Silk Road and a UNESCO World Heritage site. Explore old city walls, mausoleums and archaeological remains.

details.title: Mary – Ashgabat / Departure
details.content: Return to Ashgabat for departure or extend your journey with extra time in Turkmenistan, Uzbekistan or a wider Central Asia route.

question.question: Can this route start in Turkmenistan instead of Uzbekistan?
question.answer: Yes. The direction can be adjusted depending on your flights, visa timing and border availability.

question.question: Is this tour good for first-time Central Asia travelers?
question.answer: Yes. It combines Uzbekistan’s most famous Silk Road cities with Turkmenistan’s desert and archaeological highlights, making it a strong first regional journey.

question.question: Do I need visa support for Turkmenistan?
question.answer: Most travelers need visa or invitation support for Turkmenistan. We can guide you through the required planning steps where applicable.

question.question: Can the itinerary be customized?
question.answer: Yes. The route, hotels, pace and extensions can be adjusted based on your available days, comfort level and travel interests.

review.label: Uzbekistan and Turkmenistan were very different but worked beautifully together. The route felt balanced between culture, desert landscapes and Silk Road history.
The border and Turkmenistan planning could have been complicated, but everything was organized clearly. Darvaza and Khiva were unforgettable.
